codeReview.summary: The reviewed commit preserves the canonical shared-schema `pgtest.Open()` path and converts many bootstrap tests to unique test-owned emails, but it does not actually complete the feature contract because a previously failing bootstrap path remains broken and the async guardrails are only demonstrated in platform-only helper tests rather than integrated into an async-sensitive feature path.

issues.description: `TestImportRoutesAcceptTimeEntriesCSVUpload` is still left failing by this feature's own handoff. The feature contract explicitly required the remaining bootstrap collisions under shared-schema execution to be refactored or intentionally isolated so the previously colliding bootstrap paths no longer fail. This test was moved to `pgtest.OpenEphemeral()` but still remained broken due to importer identity mismatch, so the implementation did not finish the promised bootstrap-package remediation.

The async queue-prefix / worker-ownership / drain-to-idle requirement is only exercised in synthetic platform tests that simulate work with goroutines and a bare `JobRunner`. The reviewed commit does not wire the new async helpers into any real async-sensitive backend path such as import/export/bootstrap flows, so scrutiny still cannot verify that production-facing test lanes use test-owned queue configuration and drain-to-idle behavior concretely rather than only by demonstration scaffolding.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission shared-state files describe async queue guardrails as if they are established across the test topology, but the reviewed implementation only adds helper definitions plus platform-only demonstration tests. Workers could still assume import/export/bootstrap tests already enforce these guardrails concretely when they do not.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/library/architecture.md` and `.factory/library/environment.md` state queue-prefix / drain-to-idle rules as canonical. In code, `apps/backend/internal/testsupport/async.go` is only consumed by `apps/backend/internal/platform/async_test.go`, with no reviewed bootstrap/import/export call sites using `NewTestQueueConfig` or `DrainHelper`.

summary: Review failed again: the commit improves shared-schema safety, but it still leaves one of the previously failing bootstrap scenarios unresolved and does not make async guardrails concrete in an actual async-sensitive test path.
